free_text: Photon scattering  studies  of   the   giant   dipole
resonance in medium weight nuclei.
Electron microtron MUSL-2.
All of the targets used were metallic samples, except
for Cr-52 which was in form (Cr-52)-2(O-3),  and all
except the fe were inclosed in a thin-walled  lucite
container.  Typical  target  thickness  were  2  - 4
The relation between the number of scattered photons
detected  in  the  nai detector and the differential
cross section (d-sig)/(d-omega) is ns  =  k*(d-sig)/
(d-omega)*(omega-d)*ng*epsilon,   where   k  is  the
effective number  of  scattering  centers  per  unit
area,  omega-d  is the solid angle substended by the
detector,  epsilon is the detector  efficiency,  and
the  ng  is  the  total number of the tagged photons
incident on the target.
The statistical errors are presented only.
The corrections were  made  for  background  and  for
electronic interactions of the photons in the target
material (typically about 15%).
Quasimonochromatic photons  have been used to measure
elastic  and  inelastic  photon   scattering   cross
sections  in  the  giant  dipole resonance region of
Cr-52,  Fe, Ni-60, Mo-92, and Mo-96 in an experiment
in  which  the  elastic and inelastic scattering are
resolved. the elastic scattering cross sections show
clear  evidence  for  isospin splitting of the giant
dipole  resonance.  the  inelastic   scattering   to
low-lying vibrational levels,  which is a measure of
the coupling between the giant dipole resonance  and
collective  surface  vibrations,  is  in qualitative
agreement  with  the  predictions  of  the   dynamic
collective model.
Corrected by V.Varlamov:
4PI deleted from REACTION
and DATA-UNIT changed to MU-B/SR in SUBENTS
002 - 006, lower case, new date format.
